_doc: Locked directional expectations for the rating/composite overhaul. Evidence-backed per-dev assertions used as the validation GATE in place of Spearman ρ (self-agreement, not accuracy). Each note is tagged with the phase whose change makes the expectation hold. Excluded devs (role qa/exec) are NOT listed here — they are off the board (E). Run on the pre-fix baseline, the onset/resolved checks are EXPECTED to fail: that proves both the bug and the detector.

expectations.note: [Phase 1 / A] emergent onset. Earliest GH-actor signal VERIFIED 2026-03-06 (commit 00:23Z; first authored PR + first review also 03-06 — velocity.db). Bi-weekly periods are labeled by their START date, so Mar 6 falls in period [2026-02-23 .. 2026-03-08] → first_period=2026-02-23. Pre-fix baseline opened 2025-12-29 with ~5 phantom pre-onset periods from inherited backlog tickets; all eliminated. Threshold = period-start of his true onset, NOT Mar 1 (the original value ignored the period boundary, the cosmetic ISO-week-axis effect flagged in jira-attribution-fix-plan).
[Phase 1 / B1] 77% reporter-only resolved; assignee-only should drop him. (role=dev, stays on board.)
[Phase 1 / A+B1] role=lead, codes less; 52% reporter-only resolved removed. Elo should slide GENTLY (still plays via reviews), not fall off a cliff. THE 'don't tank leads' GUARD.
[Phase 1 / A] reviews keep him participating every period; periods played must not collapse when Jira stops gating participation.

[Phase 2 / B2] devops: 324 owned-'touched' tickets (assignee|reporter, updated in-window) but only 47 distinct issues he personally advanced through the build pipeline (dev-authored changelog transitions, verified velocity.db). Replacing passive 'touched' with actor-attributed progress removes the over-credit → he drops. The canonical 'passive owner' case from signal-audit Finding B.

[Phase 3 / B4+B5] lead-measurability check. B1 stripped his phantom reporter-resolved credit (52% reporter-only) pushing his composite down; B5 planning credit (78 issues filed, sqrt-saturated @0.25) + B4's reduced resolved weight restore it → he lands NEAR his original cohort rank (within ±1.5), now for the right reasons (real reviews + planning, not inherited filing). B5 working as intended: keeps leads on the board without letting filing volume dominate.
